channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: US troops need to stay in Syria to counter the Islamic State group, Austin says
item.description: Defense Secretary Lloyd Austin told The Associated Press that the U.S. needs to keep troops deployed in Syria to prevent the Islamic State group from reconstituting as a major threat.
item.dc:creator: Tara Copp, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: More Americans jailed in Venezuela pose a test of Trump's deal-making foreign policy
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — Venezuelan President Nicolas Maduro will be sworn in for a third term Friday while hundreds of government opponents arrested since his disputed reelection last summer languish in the country's packed prisons. Sharing cells with them are as many as 10 Americans.
item.dc:creator: JOSHUA GOODMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump provokes anxieties with Greenland, Panama Canal military talk
item.description: President-elect Trump’s refusal to rule out military action to attain his ambitions of acquiring the Panama Canal and Greenland has sparked some stern responses overseas and spurred a global debate over how seriously to take the incoming president.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Panama Canal chief dismisses Trump’s claims on China, fees
item.description: The Panama Canal Authority chief dismissed President-elect Trump’s claims that China is controlling the trade route and criticized his comments about fees. Ricaurte Vásquez Morales told The Wall Street Journal that Trump’s accusations that China is running the Panama Canal are “unfounded.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Middle East latest: Israeli hostage found dead in Gaza, military says
item.description: Israeli forces have recovered the body of a hostage held in Gaza, the military said Wednesday, adding that it was identifying additional remains that could belong to another captive.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Venezuelan opposition leader rallies support in Panama ahead of Maduro inauguration
item.description: Self-exiled Venezuelan opposition leader Edmundo González is rallying regional support for the opposition's claims that he won the July election against President Nicolás Maduro.
item.dc:creator: Juan Zamorano And Astrid Suárez, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Venezuela's ultimate political survivor Nicolás Maduro faces his toughest challenge yet
item.description: Venezuelan President Nicolás Maduro on Friday will face more international rebuke than at any time in his 12 years in power.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Canadian leaders say Trump's talk about Canada becoming the 51st state isn't funny anymore
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — U.S. President-elect Donald Trump’s comments that Canada should become the 51st state are no longer a joke and are meant to undermine America’s closest ally, Canada’s finance minister said Wednesday. Dominic LeBlanc, the country’s point person for U.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Cold spell claims first two lives in El Paso-Juarez area
item.description: Tuesday’s death of a homeless man presumably due to hypothermia has set off alarms in a border city with a large floating population of migrants.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, NewsNation

item.title: Gingrich: Trump 'trying to project' strength with Greenland talk
item.description: Former Speaker Newt Gingrich (R-Ga.) says President-elect Trump is attempting to project strength with his recent remarks about the U.S. acquiring Greenland from Denmark, comments that have not been well-received across the Atlantic.
item.dc:creator: Tristan Lejeune, The Hill

item.title: 23-year-old hiker found after surviving for 2 weeks in Australian mountain range
item.description: A medical student from Melbourne who was missing in a remote Australian mountain range for two weeks survived on two muesli bars, foraged berries and creek water, police said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A Russian missile attack in southern Ukraine has killed at least 13 civilians, officials say
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — A daytime Russian missile attack on the southern Ukraine city of Zaporizhzhia killed at least 13 civilians and injured about 30 others Wednesday, officials said.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: US strikes underground Houthi weapons depots used to hit American ships
item.description: U.S. hit weapon storage facilities used by the Iran-backed Houthi terrorist organization to target U.S. military and merchant ships in the Red Sea and Gulf of Aden.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mexico offers protection to famed singer after drug cartel death threats
item.description: Mexican authorities are offering protection to famed regional Mexican singer Natanael Cano and other artists after a drug cartel publicly threatened them, prosecutors said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tents arrive for survivors of a quake that killed 126 in freezing, high-altitude Tibet
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — Relief teams in western China shifted their focus to resettling survivors after a search Wednesday for any remaining victims of a deadly earthquake that struck a day earlier near a holy city for Tibetan Buddhists.
item.dc:creator: KEN MORITSUGU and CHRISTOPHER BODEEN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Bipartisan lawmakers introduce ‘Georgian Nightmare Non-Recognition Act' in warning to Putin allied government
item.description: Senior Republican and Democratic representatives will introduce a bill on Wednesday prohibiting recognition of the Georgian Dream government amid daily protests against the new government.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: An Italian journalist is freed from detention in Iran and returns home
item.description: An Italian journalist detained in Iran for three weeks has been freed and has returned home, after her fate had become intertwined with that of an Iranian engineer arrested in Italy and wanted by the United States.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Kim Jong Un’s big guns spotted on Russian front lines: report
item.description: A North Korean howitzer has been reported on Russia's front lines following November sightings of a shipment of two M1989 Koksan self-propelled big guns as Pyongyang entered the war against Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Senegal and Chad say ousting of French troops was their sovereign decision
item.description: DAKAR, Senegal (AP) — Senegalese and Chadian leaders said that the ousting of French troops from their territories was their sovereign decision, rejecting remarks from French President Emmanuel Macron that suggested otherwise.
item.dc:creator: MONIKA PRONCZUK and BABACAR DIONE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jean-Marie Le Pen, French far-right leader known for fiery rhetoric against immigration, dies at 96
item.description: PARIS (AP) — Jean-Marie Le Pen, the founder of France’s far-right National Front who was known for fiery rhetoric against immigration and multiculturalism that earned him both staunch supporters and widespread condemnation, died Tuesday. He was 96.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS ADAMSON, SYLVIE CORBET and ELAINE GANLEY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Earthquake 50 miles from Mount Everest leaves at least 126 dead in Tibet
item.description: A powerful magnitude 7.1 earthquake has struck Tibet, reportedly killing at least 126 on the Chinese side of the border and damaging more than 1,000 homes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Law student killed by elephant during vacation to Thailand: officials
item.description: Blanca Ojanguren, a 22-year-old student from Spain, was killed by an elephant on Friday during a bathing session at Koh Yao Elephant Care
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Venezuelan opposition leader who claimed victory over Maduro meets with Biden
item.description: Edmundo González Urrutia, the Venezuelan opposition leader who claimed to have beaten current President Nicolás Maduro in an election in July, met with President Biden on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Congo to execute over 170 people convicted of armed robbery, official says
item.description: Over 170 inmates have been flown from the Democratic Republic of the Congo's capital, Kinshasa, to a high-security prison where they will be executed, according to authorities.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump pitches 'merged' US, Canada after Trudeau resignation announcement
item.description: After Trudeau’s announcement Monday, Trump touted the idea that many Canadians would “LOVE” the idea of being the "51st State" of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Musk says UK's Farage should not be Reform party leader
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk said that British politician Nigel Farage should not be the Reform U.K. party’s leader.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: China fires shot across Trump's bow with defense sanctions
item.description: It could be a sign of a brewing tit-for-tat as the two superpowers jockey for economic and military leverage.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, NewsNation

item.title: One cigarette can decrease life expectancy by 20 minutes: Data
item.description: In 2000, data published in The BMJ estimated one cigarette would cost a person 11 minutes of their life.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation
